column1: # description: Ecological impact estimates for major LLMs — training CO2
# notes: Updated to 2026 model landscape. Most proprietary models (Claude
column3: Gemini) do not disclose parameter counts or detailed energy data. Training CO2 estimates are from published papers
column5: and third-party analyses. Inference energy from provider disclosures and third-party estimates. Many values are LOW confidence estimates or extrapolations. N/A = not publicly available. MoE models show total/active params where known.

column10: Anthropic does not disclose parameter counts or energy data. Largest Claude model; expected higher inference energy than Sonnet/Haiku.
Anthropic does not disclose parameter counts or energy data. Mid-tier model; moderate inference energy.
Anthropic does not disclose parameter counts or energy data. Smallest/fastest Claude model; lowest inference energy in family.
OpenAI does not disclose parameter counts. 1M context window model. Successor to GPT-4o with improved efficiency claims.
Smaller distilled model. Significantly lower inference energy than GPT-4.1. Designed for cost-efficient deployment.
Reasoning model with chain-of-thought. Higher inference energy per query due to extended reasoning tokens. Actual compute per query varies widely by task.
MoE architecture. Google claims continued efficiency improvements. ~0.24 Wh per median query for Gemini models broadly (Aug 2025 figure).
Flash variant optimized for speed/efficiency. Google reported 33x energy reduction over 12 months (2024-2025). Likely lowest per-query energy among Gemini models.
675B total / 41B active (MoE). Open-weight. Mistral AI previously reported ~1g CO2 per page of text generated for Large 2; Large 3 likely similar or better due to MoE efficiency.

400B total / 17B active (MoE with 128 experts). Open-weight. Very efficient inference due to low active params. Meta disclosed some training details for LLaMA 3 (539 tons CO2 for 70B); LLaMA 4 training details not yet disclosed.
235B total / 22B active (MoE). Open-weight. No public CO2 or energy data from Alibaba Cloud. Efficient inference due to MoE architecture.
671B total / 37B active (MoE). Training cost ~$5.6M using 2.788M H800 GPU-hours. Highly efficient inference due to low active params. Estimated inference CO2 extrapolated from cost and efficiency.
